title: Superconducting magnet assembly
abstract: A superconducting magnet arrangement comprises a field coil assembly with coil windings that when in operation are electrically superconducting. The field coil assembly is circuited between connection ports for a voltage supply. A switching module switches a sub- section of the field coil assembly's coil windings between its electrical superconducting and electrical resistive states, said sub-section forming a switching coil circuited between the connection ports. In the operational state where both the switching coil and the field coil(s) are superconducting and carry a permanent electrical current, the field coil(s) and the switching coil together generate a stationary magnetic field. According to the invention the switch windings give a significant contribution to the magnetic field. The field coil assembly's coil windings that may be switched between it electrically superconducting and resistive states form the switching coil. That is, the switching coil forms part of the field coil assembly and contributes significantly to the magnetic field generated by the field coil assembly.
claims: Claims CLAIMS: 1. A superconducting magnet arrangement comprising a field coil assembly with coil windings that when in operation are electrically superconducting, the field coil assembly being circuited between connection ports for a voltage supply and a switching module to switch a sub-section of the field coil assembly's coil windings between its electrical superconducting and electrical resistive states, said sub section forming a switching coil circuited between the connection ports. 2. A superconducting magnet arrangement as claimed in Claim 1, wherein the inductance of the switching coil is smaller than the inductance of the field coil assembly. 3. A superconducting magnet arrangement as claimed in anyone of the preceding Claims, and comprising a support structure which carries the field coil assembly, wherein a thermal isolation is provided between the switching coil and the support structure. 4. A superconducting magnet arrangement as claimed in anyone of the preceding Claims, wherein, when in its superconducting state the switching coil is circuited in series with the field coil assembly. 5. A superconducting magnet arrangement as claimed in anyone of the preceding Claims, wherein the field coil assembly includes multiple field coils arranged coaxially transversely to the field coilsâ windings. 6. A superconducting magnet arrangement as claimed in Claim 2, wherein the ratio of the inductance of the switching coil to the inductance of the field coil assembly in in the range of 1/20 to Â½, preferably in the range of (0.08 to 0.12). 7. A superconducting magnet arrangement as claimed in any one of the preceding Claims, wherein the field coil assemblyâs field coils and the switching coil include windings of a high-temperature superconducting material having its critical temperature in the range of 20-80K. 8. A superconducting magnet arrangement as claimed in Claim 7, wherein the high-temperature superconducting material is MgB 2 . 9. A superconducting magnet arrangement as claimed in Claim 8, wherein the switching coil includes a length of superconducting wire in the range of 1600 -2400m. 10. A method of ramping-up a field coil assembly of a superconducting magnet as claimed in anyone of Claims 1 to 9, the method comprising the steps of setting the switching coil to its resistive state and connect a voltage source to the connection ports; when the electrical current through the field coils has reached its pre-set end- value, setting the switching coil to its superconductive state and switching-off the voltage source.
